registers.name: CR1
registers.description: SPI control register 1 — configures clock phase/polarity, baud rate, master/slave, data frame format, SPI enable, and communication modes

bitfields.name: BIDIMODE
bitfields.description: Bidirectional data mode enable. 0: 2-line unidirectional, 1: 1-line bidirectional

bitfields.name: BIDIOE
bitfields.description: Output enable in bidirectional mode. 0: Output disabled (receive-only), 1: Output enabled (transmit-only). Used only when BIDIMODE=1.

bitfields.name: CRCEN
bitfields.description: Hardware CRC calculation enable. 0: CRC calculation disabled, 1: CRC calculation enabled. Note: This bit should be written only when SPI is disabled (SPE=0).

bitfields.name: CRCNEXT
bitfields.description: CRC transfer next. 0: Data phase, 1: Next transfer is CRC. Should be set before the end of last data transfer.

bitfields.name: DFF
bitfields.description: Data frame format. 0: 8-bit data frame, 1: 16-bit data frame. Note: This bit should be written only when SPI is disabled (SPE=0).

bitfields.name: RXONLY
bitfields.description: Receive only. 0: Full duplex (TX and RX), 1: Output disabled (receive-only mode). Used with BIDIMODE=0.

bitfields.name: SSM
bitfields.description: Software slave management. 0: Software slave management disabled (hardware NSS), 1: Software slave management enabled (NSS pin value replaced by SSI bit).

bitfields.name: SSI
bitfields.description: Internal slave select. This bit has an effect only when SSM=1. The value of this bit is forced onto the NSS pin and the IO value of the NSS pin is ignored.

bitfields.name: LSBFIRST
bitfields.description: Frame format. 0: MSB transmitted first, 1: LSB transmitted first. Note: This bit should not be changed when communication is ongoing.

bitfields.name: SPE
bitfields.description: SPI enable. 0: Peripheral disabled, 1: Peripheral enabled. When disabling the SPI, follow the correct disable procedure (§25.3.8).

bitfields.name: BR
bitfields.description: Baud rate control. 000: fPCLK/2, 001: fPCLK/4, 010: fPCLK/8, 011: fPCLK/16, 100: fPCLK/32, 101: fPCLK/64, 110: fPCLK/128, 111: fPCLK/256. Note: These bits should not be changed when communication is ongoing. Must be written when SPE=0.

bitfields.name: MSTR
bitfields.description: Master selection. 0: Slave configuration, 1: Master configuration. Note: This bit should not be changed when communication is ongoing. It is automatically cleared by hardware when a Mode fault (MODF) occurs.

bitfields.name: CPOL
bitfields.description: Clock polarity. 0: CK to 0 when idle, 1: CK to 1 when idle. Note: This bit should not be changed when communication is ongoing. Must be written when SPE=0.

bitfields.name: CPHA
bitfields.description: Clock phase. 0: First clock transition is first data capture edge, 1: Second clock transition is first data capture edge. Note: This bit should not be changed when communication is ongoing. Must be written when SPE=0.

registers.name: CR2
registers.description: SPI control register 2 — configures interrupt enables, SS output, and DMA enables

bitfields.name: TXEIE
bitfields.description: Tx buffer empty interrupt enable. 0: TXE interrupt masked, 1: TXE interrupt not masked. Generates interrupt when TXE flag is set.

bitfields.name: RXNEIE
bitfields.description: RX buffer not empty interrupt enable. 0: RXNE interrupt masked, 1: RXNE interrupt not masked. Generates interrupt when RXNE flag is set.

bitfields.name: ERRIE
bitfields.description: Error interrupt enable. 0: Error interrupt masked, 1: Error interrupt not masked. Generates interrupt when CRCERR, OVR, or MODF flag is set.

bitfields.name: SSOE
bitfields.description: SS output enable. 0: SS output disabled in master mode, multimaster capability enabled. 1: SS output enabled in master mode, cannot work in multimaster environment.

bitfields.name: TXDMAEN
bitfields.description: Tx buffer DMA enable. 0: Tx buffer DMA disabled, 1: Tx buffer DMA enabled. When set, a DMA request is generated whenever the TXE flag is set.

bitfields.name: RXDMAEN
bitfields.description: Rx buffer DMA enable. 0: Rx buffer DMA disabled, 1: Rx buffer DMA enabled. When set, a DMA request is generated whenever the RXNE flag is set.

registers.name: SR
registers.description: SPI status register — contains transmission/reception status flags and error indicators. Mixed access types: RO, W0C, RC_SEQ.

bitfields.name: BSY
bitfields.description: Busy flag. 0: SPI not busy, 1: SPI is busy in communication or Tx buffer is not empty. Set and cleared by hardware. Do not use BSY flag alone to handle each data transmission or reception — use TXE and RXNE flags instead.

bitfields.name: OVR
bitfields.description: Overrun flag. Set by hardware when data is received and the previous data has not yet been read from SPI_DR. Cleared by a read access to SPI_DR followed by a read access to SPI_SR.

bitfields.name: MODF
bitfields.description: Mode fault. Set by hardware when NSS is pulled low in master mode with SSM=0, or SSI=0 with SSM=1. Cleared by a software sequence: read SPI_SR when MODF bit is set, then write to SPI_CR1.

bitfields.name: CRCERR
bitfields.description: CRC error flag. Set by hardware when the CRC value received does not match the SPI_RXCRCR value. Cleared by writing 0 to this bit (rc_w0).

bitfields.name: UDR
bitfields.description: Underrun flag (I2S mode only). Not used in SPI mode.

bitfields.name: CHSIDE
bitfields.description: Channel side (I2S mode only). Not used in SPI mode.

bitfields.name: TXE
bitfields.description: Transmit buffer empty. 0: Tx buffer not empty, 1: Tx buffer empty. Set by hardware when the Tx buffer is empty. Cleared by writing to SPI_DR.

bitfields.name: RXNE
bitfields.description: Receive buffer not empty. 0: Rx buffer empty, 1: Rx buffer not empty. Set by hardware when the Rx buffer contains valid data. Cleared by reading SPI_DR.

registers.name: DR
registers.description: SPI data register — used for transmitting and receiving data. When DFF=0 (8-bit), only bits [7:0] are used. When DFF=1 (16-bit), all 16 bits are used.

bitfields.name: DR
bitfields.description: Data register. Contains the received data or the data to be transmitted.

registers.name: CRCPR
registers.description: SPI CRC polynomial register — contains the polynomial for CRC calculation. Must be odd (bit 0 = 1). Should be written only when SPI is disabled (SPE=0).

bitfields.name: CRCPOLY
bitfields.description: CRC polynomial register. Default value is 0x0007 (x^3+x^2+x).

registers.name: RXCRCR
registers.description: SPI Rx CRC register — contains the computed CRC value of received bytes. Cleared when CRCEN bit is written to 1.

bitfields.name: RXCRC
bitfields.description: Rx CRC register. Contains the CRC calculated on the received bytes.

registers.name: TXCRCR
registers.description: SPI Tx CRC register — contains the computed CRC value of transmitted bytes. Cleared when CRCEN bit is written to 1.

bitfields.name: TXCRC
bitfields.description: Tx CRC register. Contains the CRC calculated on the transmitted bytes.

interrupts.name: SPI1
interrupts.description: SPI1 global interrupt. Shared by TXE, RXNE, MODF, OVR, and CRCERR events.

interrupts.name: SPI2
interrupts.description: SPI2 global interrupt. Shared by TXE, RXNE, MODF, OVR, and CRCERR events.

atomic_sequences.name: Overrun Flag Clear Sequence

steps.comment: Read data register to discard stale data
Read status register to complete OVR flag clear

constraints: Steps must be executed in order
No intervening SPI register access between steps

atomic_sequences.name: Mode Fault Flag Clear Sequence

steps.comment: Read SR while MODF is set
Write to CR1 to complete the MODF clear sequence

constraints: Read SR must occur while MODF=1
After clearing, reconfigure SPI as needed since MSTR was cleared by hardware

errors.effect: New received data overwrites unread data in Rx buffer. Subsequent reception is blocked until OVR is cleared.
errors.prevention: Read DR before next data frame arrives. Use DMA for high-speed transfers.

errors.effect: MSTR bit is cleared by hardware, SPI forced to slave mode. SPE is not cleared. NSS pin released.
errors.prevention: In software NSS mode (SSM=1), set SSI=1 for master (INV_SPI_001). In hardware NSS multimaster mode, implement arbitration protocol.

errors.effect: CRC value received does not match computed CRC. Data integrity compromised.
errors.clear_method: Write 0 to CRCERR bit (SR &= ~CRCERR_Msk). INV_SPI_004: CRCERR is W0C, must use &= ~Msk, never |=.
errors.prevention: Ensure matching CRC polynomial on both master and slave. Verify DFF consistency.

init_sequence.description: Enable SPI peripheral clock via RCC
Enable GPIO port clock and AFIO clock (if remap needed)
Configure AFIO remap if using alternate pin mapping (SPI1 only)
Configure GPIO pins for SPI function (SCK, MISO, MOSI, NSS per Table 25)
Ensure SPI is disabled (SPE=0) before configuration — guard for LOCK fields
Configure clock polarity and phase (CPOL, CPHA)
Configure master/slave mode (MSTR), data frame format (DFF), bit order (LSBFIRST), and communication mode (BIDIMODE, BIDIOE, RXONLY)
init_sequence.field: MSTR, DFF, LSBFIRST, BIDIMODE, BIDIOE, RXONLY

init_sequence.description: Configure NSS management (SSM, SSI) and CRC (CRCEN, CRCPR)
Configure interrupt enables (TXEIE, RXNEIE, ERRIE) and DMA enables (TXDMAEN, RXDMAEN) and SS output (SSOE)
init_sequence.field: TXEIE, RXNEIE, ERRIE, SSOE, TXDMAEN, RXDMAEN

init_sequence.description: Enable SPI peripheral (SPE=1) — must be last configuration step

deinit_sequence.description: Disable SPI clock via RCC (optional, for power saving)

full_duplex_master_tx_rx.description: Disable procedure for master in full-duplex or transmit-only mode
steps: 1. Wait until RXNE=1 (last received frame available in Rx buffer), then read one byte from DR to drain it. Ensures no data loss before transmission completes.
2. Wait until TXE=1 (last data loaded into Tx buffer)
full_duplex_master_tx_rx.note: Applies to both master and slave in full-duplex or transmit-only modes. Same procedure regardless of MSTR setting.

master_receive_only.description: Disable procedure for master in receive-only mode (RXONLY=1)
steps: 1. Clear SPE bit (stops clock generation after current reception completes)
master_receive_only.note: Applies to both master and slave in receive-only mode. SPE must be cleared first to stop clock generation.

bidirectional_rx.description: Disable procedure for bidirectional receive mode (BIDIMODE=1, BIDIOE=0)
steps: 1. Clear BIDIOE bit to stop clock in master mode / stop driving data in slave mode
bidirectional_rx.note: Applies to both master and slave in bidirectional receive mode.

operation_modes.name: full_duplex_master
operation_modes.description: Full-duplex master mode. 2-line unidirectional, simultaneous TX and RX.

operation_modes.name: full_duplex_slave
operation_modes.description: Full-duplex slave mode. 2-line unidirectional, simultaneous TX and RX.

operation_modes.name: receive_only_master
operation_modes.description: Receive-only master mode. Master generates clock, only receives data on MISO. MOSI not used.
operation_modes.note: Clock runs continuously until SPE is cleared. Use specific disable procedure.

operation_modes.name: receive_only_slave
operation_modes.description: Receive-only slave mode. Slave only receives data on MOSI. Does not drive MISO.

operation_modes.name: bidirectional_tx_master
operation_modes.description: Bidirectional master, transmit mode. Single data line (MOSI) used for TX.

operation_modes.name: bidirectional_rx_master
operation_modes.description: Bidirectional master, receive mode. Single data line (MISO) used for RX.
operation_modes.note: Clock runs continuously in receive mode. Use BIDIOE disable procedure.

operation_modes.name: bidirectional_tx_slave
operation_modes.description: Bidirectional slave, transmit mode. Single data line (MISO) used for TX.

operation_modes.name: bidirectional_rx_slave
operation_modes.description: Bidirectional slave, receive mode. Single data line (MOSI) used for RX.

error_handling: OVR (Overrun): Set when DR not read before next data received. Clear with SEQ_OVR_CLEAR (read DR, read SR).
MODF (Mode Fault): Set when NSS pulled low in master mode. MSTR auto-cleared. Clear with SEQ_MODF_CLEAR (read SR, write CR1). Reconfigure as master if needed.
CRCERR (CRC Error): Set when CRC mismatch detected. Clear by writing 0 to CRCERR bit (W0C). Retransmit data.
UDR (Underrun, I2S only): Not applicable in SPI mode.

invariants.description: In master mode with software slave management (SSM=1), SSI must be set to 1. If SSI=0, hardware detects NSS low (because SSI replaces the NSS pin value), triggers MODF, and clears MSTR — SPI degrades to slave mode.
invariants.violation_effect: MSTR cleared by hardware, SR.MODF set, SPI forced to slave mode

invariants.description: BR, CPOL, CPHA, DFF, CRCEN, LSBFIRST are LOCK fields — must be configured when SPE=0. Writing these while SPE=1 is undefined behavior.
invariants.violation_effect: Undefined behavior (writes may be silently ignored or cause incorrect operation)

invariants.description: SPI must not be disabled while BSY=1. Wait for TXE=1 and BSY=0 before clearing SPE, otherwise the last frame may be corrupted.
invariants.violation_effect: Last data frame may be corrupted or lost

invariants.description: CRCERR is W0C (rc_w0) — cleared by writing 0. Must use '&= ~CRCERR_Msk' pattern. Never use '|=' on SR register.
invariants.violation_effect: W0C bit not cleared if wrong write pattern used

configuration_strategies.name: Single-Master Mode with Software NSS
configuration_strategies.description: Standard single-master configuration. Master uses software NSS management (SSM=1, SSI=1). NSS pin free for GPIO use or directly controlled as chip-select via GPIO output.
prerequisites: NSS pin configured as GPIO output for chip-select (or unused)
Master manually drives GPIO NSS low before communication, high after
constraints: SSI must be 1 (INV_SPI_001) to prevent MODF
SSOE must be 0 (not needed in software NSS mode)

configuration_strategies.name: Single-Master Mode with Hardware NSS Output
configuration_strategies.description: Master uses hardware-managed NSS output (SSOE=1). NSS driven low automatically when SPE=1, high when SPE=0. Simplest master config but ties NSS to SPI enable state.
prerequisites: NSS pin configured as Alternate Function Push-Pull output
Only one slave connected (NSS stays low throughout enabled period)
constraints: Cannot work in multimaster environment (SSOE=1 disables multimaster)
NSS goes low when SPE=1 and high when SPE=0 — no per-transfer control

configuration_strategies.name: Multimaster Mode with Collision Arbitration
configuration_strategies.description: Multiple masters share the SPI bus. Each master monitors NSS input for collision detection via MODF flag. When MODF fires, the losing master releases the bus.
prerequisites: NSS pin configured as Input Floating (hardware NSS input for collision detection)
MODF interrupt enabled (CR2.ERRIE=1) for collision notification
constraints: SSOE must be 0 (SSOE=1 would disable multimaster capability)
When MODF detected: MSTR is auto-cleared by hardware, must reconfigure
Each master must drive NSS of other masters low only during its own transmission

configuration_strategies.name: Slave Mode with Hardware NSS
configuration_strategies.description: Slave uses hardware NSS management. Slave is selected when external master pulls NSS pin low. Deselected when NSS goes high.
prerequisites: NSS pin configured as Input Floating
constraints: Slave active only when NSS is low
Data transmission/reception gated by external NSS signal

configuration_strategies.name: Slave Mode with Software NSS
configuration_strategies.description: Slave uses software NSS management (SSM=1). The SSI bit value replaces the NSS pin level. SSI=0 selects the slave, SSI=1 deselects it. NSS pin freed for other use.
prerequisites: Application manages slave selection via SSI bit
NSS pin available for other GPIO use
constraints: SSI=0 means slave is always selected (unless software changes SSI)
If SSI set to 1 while slave is communicating, slave is deselected

cross_field_constraints.title: Master + Software NSS requires SSI=1 (INV_SPI_001)
cross_field_constraints.description: When MSTR=1 and SSM=1, SSI must be 1. If SSI=0, hardware interprets NSS as low (via software override), triggers MODF fault, and auto-clears MSTR — SPI degrades to slave mode.
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: MODF flag set by hardware; MSTR automatically cleared; SPI forced to slave mode

cross_field_constraints.title: BR[2:0] must not be modified when SPE=1
cross_field_constraints.description: BR (baud rate) is a LOCK field. Must be written only when SPE=0. Writing BR while SPE=1 results in undefined behavior.
procedure: 2. If true, disable SPI following proper disable procedure (wait TXE=1, BSY=0, then clear SPE)
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: Undefined behavior — baud rate change may not take effect or cause incorrect clocking

cross_field_constraints.title: CPOL/CPHA must not be modified when SPE=1
cross_field_constraints.description: CPOL and CPHA are LOCK fields. Must be written only when SPE=0. Changing clock polarity/phase during active communication causes data corruption.
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: Undefined behavior — clock phase/polarity change during communication corrupts data

cross_field_constraints.title: DFF and CRCEN must not be modified when SPE=1
cross_field_constraints.description: DFF (data frame format) and CRCEN must be written only when SPI is disabled (SPE=0). DFF change also requires CRC re-initialization if CRCEN is used.
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: Undefined behavior — frame format or CRC configuration corrupted

cross_field_constraints.title: DFF change requires CRC re-initialization
cross_field_constraints.description: When changing DFF (8-bit to 16-bit or vice versa), CRC registers (RXCRCR/TXCRCR) retain stale values. Must toggle CRCEN off then on to reset CRC calculation.
procedure: 2. Clear CRCEN (CR1.CRCEN = 0) to flush CRC state
4. Set CRCEN = 1 (if CRC calculation is needed)
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: CRC mismatch detection fails; stale CRC values cause spurious CRCERR flags

cross_field_constraints.title: SPI disable requires BSY=0 and TXE=1 (INV_SPI_003)
cross_field_constraints.description: Before clearing SPE to disable SPI, must wait for TXE=1 (Tx buffer empty) and BSY=0 (no ongoing transfer). Premature disable corrupts the last frame.
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: Last data frame may be corrupted or lost; SPI left in inconsistent state

cross_field_constraints.title: CRCERR must be cleared with &= ~Msk (W0C safety, INV_SPI_004)
cross_field_constraints.description: SR is a mixed-access register. CRCERR is W0C (rc_w0). OVR and MODF are RC_SEQ. Never use '|=' on SR register. For CRCERR, use 'SR &= ~CRCERR_Msk'. For OVR/MODF, use their respective atomic clear sequences.
cross_field_constraints.precondition: write_to(SR) must respect per-bitfield access types
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: Using |= on SR may inadvertently affect W0C or RC_SEQ flags; CRCERR not properly cleared

cross_field_constraints.title: RCC -> AFIO -> GPIO -> SPI configuration order
cross_field_constraints.description: Initialization must follow strict order: (1) Enable RCC clocks for SPI, GPIO, AFIO. (2) Configure AFIO remap if needed (SPI1 only). (3) Configure GPIO pins per Table 25. (4) Configure SPI registers.
procedure: 1. Enable RCC clocks (SPI peripheral + GPIO port + AFIO if remap)
2. Set AFIO_MAPR.SPI1_REMAP if alternate pins needed
3. Configure GPIO pins (CNF + MODE per Table 25)
cross_field_constraints.violation_effect: GPIO configured on wrong physical pins; SPI peripheral not clocked; communication fails silently

errata.title: STM32F103 SPI Errata — pending ES0306 review
errata.description: The STM32F103 errata sheet (ES0306) should be checked for known SPI issues. Common issues on STM32F1 SPI include BSY flag behavior and CRC calculation edge cases.

pending_reviews.question: Does ES0306 (STM32F103 errata) contain SPI-specific errata that affect register access patterns or initialization sequences?
pending_reviews.context: ES0306 is the official errata sheet for STM32F103. Known STM32F1 SPI issues may affect BSY flag reliability and CRC behavior.
options: SPI errata exists — update IR accordingly

pending_reviews.question: Is LSBFIRST also a LOCK field (must be written when SPE=0)? The manual says 'should not be changed when communication is ongoing' but does not explicitly say 'must be written when SPE=0'.
pending_reviews.context: RM0008 §25.5.1 p.715 — LSBFIRST description says not to change during communication, but the note about SPE=0 requirement is only explicit for BR/CPOL/CPHA/DFF/CRCEN.
options: LSBFIRST is a LOCK field (SPE=0 required)
LSBFIRST can be changed when not actively communicating (BSY=0 sufficient)
pending_reviews.human_decision: LSBFIRST is a LOCK field (SPE=0 required)

generation_metadata.notes: STM32F103C8T6 is medium-density (64KB Flash, 20KB SRAM, LQFP48). Has SPI1 (APB2) and SPI2 (APB1). No SPI3 (only on high/XL-density and connectivity line). Errata ES0306 pending review.
